context: Tests correct onLeave implementation for a casual mobile game: checks the consented flag to distinguish voluntary from involuntary disconnect, uses allowReconnection with an appropriate timeout (20-30s for casual), and only removes the player if the reconnection window expires.

checklist.name: Checks consented flag
checklist.description: onLeave inspects the consented parameter to branch between voluntary and involuntary disconnect

checklist.name: allowReconnection called
checklist.description: Non-consented path calls this.allowReconnection(client, timeout) — not this.state.players.delete() immediately

checklist.name: Timeout in 20-30s range
checklist.description: The timeout passed to allowReconnection is between 20 and 30 seconds (appropriate for a casual match)

checklist.name: Conditional removal
checklist.description: Player is deleted from state only after allowReconnection resolves to falsy (timeout expired) — not before

checklist.name: Consented remove immediately
checklist.description: Voluntary disconnect (consented === true) removes the player from state without calling allowReconnection

checklist.name: onLeave is async
checklist.description: onLeave is declared as an async function (required for await this.allowReconnection)

checklist.name: All hooks present
checklist.description: Room implements onCreate, onJoin, onLeave, and onDispose
